summary:
Trichotillomania occurs significantly more frequently in women than men, with an established female-to-male ratio of around 9:1. It is therefore not encountered with equal frequency across both sexes.

The provided information indicates that trichotillomania affects females much more often than males due to its consistent male-female prevalence ratio of roughly 9:1. Hence, it does not occur at equal frequencies among individuals regardless of sex.\boxed{\text{no}}
Trichotillomania shows significant sexual dimorphism, being markedly more frequent in females compared to males based on multiple sources citing a ~9:1 female-preference rate. As such, no—trichotillomania is not found equally in males and females.
